story.title: COVID-19 Oral History with Dave Jeppesen
story.description: This transcript is an interview with Dave Jefferson, Director of the Idaho Department of Health and Welfare, conducted by State Historian Hannah Laurie Hine. The discussion primarily centers on Jefferson's response to the COVID-19 pandemic as part of Governor Brad Little's Coronavirus Working Group and the Idaho Department of Health and Welfare's efforts. Topics include Jefferson's career trajectory, the philosophy of public health, and the department's structure and services. The conversation spans implementing and managing COVID-19 response measures, such as contact tracing, vaccination distribution, and communicating effectively with the public. Challenges in legislative relations, operational hurdles, and strategies for managing public health during the pandemic were also discussed. The interview ends with Jefferson reflecting on leadership lessons and the pandemic's personal impact.
